title: Magnitudes are older than numerals
caption: Length exists before its label.
role: Separate the raw geometric thing from the later numeric report. The learner should feel that length exists before counting.
prompt_core: On a warm drafting table, show three unlabeled objects whose lengths can be felt immediately: a wooden dowel, a loop of rope pulled straight, and the edge of a steel square. No numerals anywhere. A faint translucent geometric overlay should reveal that all three are instances of extension between endpoints, with one object cast in sharp focus and the others echoing it as physical thing, relation, and eventual measurement. Serious editorial illustration, tactile materials, calm lighting, generous negative space, adult and elegant, not schoolish.

title: Congruence: sameness without counting
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: Sameness is what survives the allowed motion.
overlay: Light vector overlays for endpoints and motion arrows recommended.
style: hybrid diagrammatic plate with tactile materials
role: Make sameness feel operational: two lengths are the same because rigid motion can carry one onto the other.
prompt_core: Create a precise yet artistic scene of two line segments on translucent vellum being slid and rotated until they coincide perfectly. Include a brass compass, a rigid metal rod, and a ghosted sequence of motions showing slide, rotation, and flip, with the final perfect overlap glowing subtly. The image should communicate preservation under rigid motion more strongly than any ruler would. No text embedded; keep geometry clean enough for optional vector labels later.

title: The first honest act of measurement: choose a unit
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: Object, standard, comparison, report are not the same thing.
overlay: Best as AI base plus vector overlay for arrows and tick marks.
role: Show measurement as a pipeline from thing to chosen standard to ratio to written label.
prompt_core: Compose a four-stage visual pipeline moving left to right: an unlabeled segment as raw magnitude, a deliberately chosen reference stick highlighted in brass as the unit, a comparison stage where copies of the unit are being laid against the segment, and finally a clean symbolic tag floating as the last compressed report. Make clear that the chosen unit is itself a length while the comparison ratio is abstract. Elegant editorial infographic feel, but painterly enough to feel like art rather than software UI. No text inside the image except optional simple tick marks that can be added later.

title: Comparison before multiplication
caption: Comparison and concatenation come before multiplication.
role: Anchor arithmetic in simpler acts: compare, concatenate, compare again.
prompt_core: Show three stripped-down wooden sticks on a dark tabletop. One pair is placed end to end to form a longer combined length, while a third lies nearby as the comparison target. The composition should make it visually obvious that you can decide shorter, longer, or equal without any numbers at all. Add subtle ghosted hands or transparent overlays suggesting the act of placing and joining. Quiet, rigorous, almost Euclidean in spirit.

title: What school usually hides here
caption: Do not confuse the object with the report about the object.
role: Correct the common collapse of point, distance, unit, coordinate, and signed displacement into one blur.
prompt_core: Design a split-panel image. On the left, a blurred chaotic classroom-style collapse: point, ruler mark, arrow, unit stick, and coordinate label visually tangled together. On the right, the same five layers separated into clean stacked transparent planes: a point, a segment, a chosen unit, a coordinate readout, and a directed arrow. Make the right side crisp, humane, and relieving, as if mental fog has lifted. Sophisticated graphic-editorial style, adult mathematics book, no childish icons.

title: Historical note: why the Greeks cared so much
caption: Irrationality forced a deeper foundation than mere calculation.
role: Humanize the historical pressure that pushed mathematics from computation toward justification.
prompt_core: Create an atmospheric scene in the ancient Mediterranean at golden hour: survey ropes, tablets, carved stone diagrams, and two thinkers confronting a square and its diagonal with visible seriousness. The mood should suggest a civilization moving from practical measurement toward proof because certain lengths refuse to fit tidy counting. Avoid costume-drama cliché; make it feel like real intellectual pressure, not mythology. Dust, sunlight, limestone, disciplined composition.

title: Thought experiments
caption: Switch the standard, not the thing, and watch what changes.
role: Invite the reader to rehearse the section with physical imagination rather than procedure.
prompt_core: Make a contemplative desk scene arranged like a mental exercise sheet without words: two pencils being compared by overlay, a unit stick swapped for a different longer unit, and a measured object remaining unchanged while only the report shifts. The scene should feel sparse and meditative, with each object separated enough that a reader can mentally run the experiment. Soft top light, minimal clutter, almost monastic clarity.

title: Common confusion to kill early
caption: A thing, a standard, and a number are related but not interchangeable.
role: Visually kill the conflation of magnitude, unit, and numeral before it spreads into later chapters.
prompt_core: Show a single table edge or rod seen through three transparent stacked panels. The bottom panel is the actual magnitude, the middle panel is a chosen unit repeatedly compared against it, and the top panel is a clean symbolic token hovering like a caption. The misreading to avoid should be visually implied by trying to collapse the layers into one flat mistaken image at the far edge. Elegant, layered, high-contrast, concept-first.

title: A line becomes a number line only after choices
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: Coordinates need a frame before they can speak.
overlay: Vector overlay for origin and arrows recommended.
role: Show that a number line is not given by nature; it is created by fixing an origin, a direction, and a unit.
prompt_core: Illustrate a bare horizontal line gradually becoming a number line through three deliberate interventions: a hand pins an origin, an arrow establishes positive direction, and a brass unit segment is stepped off to set scale. The composition should make the frame-building act visible, almost like preparing a stage before a performance. Crisp linework, subtle depth, restrained palette of graphite and blue, plenty of room for later labels.

title: Directed segments: where negatives actually come from
caption: Negative means opposite orientation, not spooky less-than-nothing.
style: symmetry on a line with embodied arrows
role: Replace the vague idea of 'below zero' with opposition of direction.
prompt_core: Show an origin at center with equal arrows extending right and left, the left-pointing arrow rendered as the exact directional reversal of the right-pointing one. Add a faint half-turn arc around the origin to suggest that negation is a reversal symmetry, not a mystical anti-number. The scene should feel spatially honest and almost physical, as though arrows are rigid displacements on a rail. Sophisticated, spare, no text.

title: Integers as repeated unit translations
caption: An integer can be seen as repeated copies of one unit move.
role: Make integer positions feel like accumulated copies of one repeated move.
prompt_core: Create a rhythmic sequence of identical unit arrows stepping across a line from the origin, each new endpoint inheriting the last. The image should feel like a drumbeat of equal translations: same move, same move, same move. Consider subtle footprints, mechanical ticks, or repeated light pulses to reinforce the idea of composed repetition. Clean, adult, visually musical without becoming playful.

title: Another lens: balance sheets and elevators
caption: Different stories, one structure: reference plus orientation.
role: Offer alternate embodied analogies for signed quantity that are not purely geometric.
prompt_core: Make a refined triptych: an elevator shaft with floors above and below a reference floor, a ledger with gains and debts arranged around a zero balance line, and a road marker with east-west displacements from a signpost. All three scenes should quietly rhyme compositionally so the reader sees one underlying structure wearing three costumes. Elegant editorial style, subdued palette, no cartoon office clichés.

title: Historical note: why negatives felt suspicious
caption: Negatives became natural only after people saw them as structured opposites.
role: Show the cultural unevenness of accepting negatives.
prompt_core: Create a three-part historical collage that moves from Chinese counting rods to an Indian merchant or mathematician handling debts and fortunes, then to a skeptical early modern European scholar staring uneasily at a negative result on paper. Keep the tone respectful and intellectually alive rather than touristy. Use shared compositional geometry so the viewer senses the idea traveling across time and cultures before becoming accepted.

title: The dimension subtlety
caption: The coordinate and the segment it reports are not identical.
role: Disentangle pure coordinate value from the actual segment of that many units.
prompt_core: Show a single point on a line with two distinct but related visual layers: one layer is the pure coordinate readout, the other is the actual directed segment from origin to that point. A chosen unit segment sits nearby as the hidden mediator. The left side of the image should tempt a collapse of these layers; the right side should cleanly separate them. Transparent planes, precise composition, concept-forward design.

title: Thought experiments
caption: Change the frame and the report changes; the place stays put.
role: Let the reader mentally run origin flips and unit changes.
prompt_core: Create a quiet scene with footsteps on a floor grid moving east then west, alongside a line where the positive arrow flips direction and the coordinate labels would all reverse if added. Include a subtle second panel where the unit changes and the same location receives a different report. Minimal, contemplative, almost like an architect’s margin sketch for a live thought experiment.

title: A preview of later structure
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
caption: Small symmetries grow into larger mathematics.
role: Preview translation, reflection, and scaling as the seed of later mathematics.
prompt_core: Design a wide bridge image starting from a simple number line at the left and branching upward into three luminous pathways labeled only by visual action: sliding translations, mirror reflections, and origin-centered scalings. Those paths should then fade into richer later worlds—vectors, complex rotations, spacetime grids—without becoming busy. The point is to show that the humble number line already contains the seed of a vast transformation language.

title: Addition is composition of translations
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: Addition is what happens when moves are chained together.
overlay: Vector arrows and labels can be overlaid later.
role: Make addition feel like path-composition, not symbol pushing.
prompt_core: Show a point traveling through space by two consecutive arrows, first to an intermediate location and then to a final one, while a larger resultant arrow spans directly from start to finish. The geometry should be crisp, but the scene can feel slightly cinematic, with ghosted traces and luminous path memory. The viewer should immediately understand that addition is doing one move after another.

title: Why commutativity is not a coincidence
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: The same endpoint from two orders is a geometric symmetry.
overlay: Use vector overlay for exact arrowheads and optional labels.
role: Reveal commutativity as a symmetry of flat-space translations.
prompt_core: Create a clean but beautiful illustration of two vectors in the plane forming a parallelogram, with two faint motion sequences showing a-then-b and b-then-a landing at the same corner. The important feeling is inevitability: the diagonal is not a mnemonic trick but the natural summary of flat-space motion. Refined blueprint lines combined with soft depth and elegant color restraint.

title: Subtraction is composition with the inverse move
caption: Subtraction is addition of the move that undoes.
role: Show subtraction as undoing rather than removal mysticism.
prompt_core: Illustrate a displacement arrow and its exact inverse as equal rigid moves in opposite directions, perhaps on a smooth rail or floor grid. Then show a path where one move is followed by the undoing move, returning to the start with a calm closed loop. The image should make subtraction feel like composition with an inverse action, not like taking away apples.

title: Associativity: grouping does not change the total path
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: Different groupings, same accumulated displacement.
role: Show grouping differences as invisible to the final motion outcome.
prompt_core: Compose an elegant three-step path in space with translucent brackets grouping the first two moves in one version and the last two in another version, yet both sequences collapse onto the same final trajectory. The visual emphasis should be on endpoint invariance under regrouping. Use ghosted arcs or subtle brace shapes rather than text. Sophisticated motion-structure illustration.

title: Another lens: addition as bookkeeping of change
caption: Addition also lives in cumulative change, not only in space.
role: Offer a non-spatial lens that still preserves structure.
prompt_core: Create a refined editorial image of a ledger or dashboard of gains and losses stacking over time, with positive and negative changes accumulating into one net result. Avoid generic finance imagery; keep it abstract and structural, like bookkeeping as geometry. The visual rhyme with arrows should be present through vertical and horizontal accumulation traces, showing that motion and accounting share the same algebraic skeleton.

title: Historical note: from counting to structure
caption: Operations became deeper when people asked what laws they obey.
role: Locate the shift from arithmetic procedure to abstract operation.
prompt_core: Show a visual evolution from pebbles or tallies used for counting, to written algorithms on parchment, to a modern clean abstraction of translations on a line. The image should feel like the concept of addition maturing from a practical tallying act into an operation with identity, inverse, and symmetry. Keep the montage tight and coherent, not a noisy timeline.

title: Thought experiments
caption: Run the move sequence in your head and trust the structure you see.
role: Let the reader test commutativity and inverse structure mentally.
prompt_core: Depict a small robot or marker on a clean floor grid receiving two move instructions in alternate orders, both arriving at the same square; nearby, another tiny sequence goes out and then back to start. Make it feel like a quiet lab sketch rather than a cute robot cartoon. Sparse, crisp, and mentally runnable at a glance.

title: Equal partition is the key act
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: Equal partition is built, not merely eyeballed.
overlay: Hybrid mode: precise parallels and tick marks can be overlaid.
role: Show equal partition as a genuine geometric achievement, not a casual visual guess.
prompt_core: Illustrate a segment being divided by a classical geometric construction: an auxiliary ray, equal compass steps on that ray, a connector to the segment endpoint, and parallel lines transporting equal spacing back onto the original segment. The scene should feel like craft knowledge becoming rigor. Use precise drafting-table aesthetics with tactile paper and brass instruments.

title: One over n is an actual constructed length
caption: One over n names a real constructed magnitude.
role: Give 1/n physical dignity as a built segment, not a pizza-slice slogan.
prompt_core: Show the unit segment transformed into several equal parts, with one single part lifted visually into prominence like a crafted object of its own. The image should make that small segment feel legitimate and exact, not a disposable fraction piece. Elegant composition, subtle shadowing, strong sense of the part inheriting reality from the whole.

title: m/n is repeated taking of a constructed part
caption: Fractions can be built from equal parts and repetition.
role: Make m/n feel like repeated concatenation of a built subunit.
prompt_core: Create a sequence where one equal fraction-part is repeated end to end until a longer segment emerges, with the repeated piece clearly identical each time. Let the repetition read almost musically or architecturally, like modular construction. Keep the image clean and adult, emphasizing that m over n is made from a real piece taken repeatedly.

title: Equivalent fractions are the same magnitude wearing different descriptions
caption: Different fraction stories can point to the same length.
role: Show symbolic plurality collapsing onto one congruent magnitude.
prompt_core: Design an image in which several differently partitioned units—halves, quarters, hundredths—each highlight a selected total that lands on exactly the same resulting segment. The partitions can differ dramatically in granularity, but the extracted length should align perfectly in one final shared overlay. The viewer should feel the relief of sameness underneath multiple descriptions.

title: Commensurable versus incommensurable
caption: Some lengths share a measure; others refuse every common unit.
role: Visualize the difference between sharing a common measure and refusing one.
prompt_core: Create a two-panel composition. In one panel, two segments are neatly tiled by repeated copies of a smaller common unit. In the other, a square’s side and diagonal resist exact common tiling no matter how small the trial unit becomes, with a subtle sense of stubborn mismatch. Keep the mood precise, not mystical. This is the first glimpse of the irrational problem.

title: Another lens: music and rhythm
caption: Fractions live in time as well as space.
role: Translate fraction structure into time and pulse for learners who feel rhythm before geometry.
prompt_core: Show a refined rhythmic grid with pulses dividing a measure into halves, thirds, quarters, and syncopated patterns, visually matched to divided line segments nearby. The link should be structural: equal time partitions and equal spatial partitions are one idea in two media. No cartoon instruments; think elegant score fragments, handclap marks, resonant minimalism.

title: Historical note: why fractions mattered
caption: Fractions were invented because life refused to arrive in wholes.
role: Ground fractions in concrete human pressures like allocation and measurement.
prompt_core: Create a historically grounded montage of grain being portioned, a field boundary being measured, a recipe or vessel being divided, and a sky chart requiring fractional precision. The scenes should read as one civilization-level argument for why exact wholes were never enough. Textures of clay, linen, grain, and measured rope; serious and practical rather than quaint.

title: Thought experiments
caption: Construction power depends on the allowed moves.
role: Invite the reader to mentally test what tools can and cannot construct.
prompt_core: Show a blank straightedge failing to mark one third directly, then an auxiliary ray and parallels suddenly making the division possible. Keep the composition minimal, like a marginal note in an architect’s sketchbook. The image should provoke the question: what operations are available, and what new powers do they unlock?

title: Common confusion
caption: A fraction can be a magnitude or a ratio, depending on the role.
role: Differentiate the fraction as length from the fraction as pure ratio.
prompt_core: Design a layered image where the same fraction notation is seen in two valid roles: as a selected subsegment of a unit line, and as a dimensionless comparison between magnitudes. Use transparent overlays and color-coding so the two lives are distinct but visibly connected. The goal is to prevent later dimensional confusion while honoring both uses.

title: Why this section is load-bearing
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
caption: This chapter is the hidden foundation under the later machine.
role: Signal that proportion theory is the hidden foundation beneath later similarity and multiplication.
prompt_core: Create an image of a stone geometric edifice whose visible upper structure—similar triangles, multiplication, calculus machinery—rests on a deeper hidden foundation slab made of repeated integer comparisons and balance tests. It should feel like the reader is being shown the basement of the subject for the first time. Classical gravity, clean symbolic hints, no melodrama.

title: The Eudoxus definition
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: Equal ratios survive every integer-scaled comparison.
overlay: Hybrid mode strongly recommended for exact multiple stacks and symbols.
role: Translate a difficult verbal definition into a memorable discrimination machine.
prompt_core: Visualize Eudoxus proportion as two paired comparison tracks being tested by many integer multiples. Imagine stacked copies of segments A and B on one side and C and D on the other, with each trial pair m and n sending both sides into the same greater-than, equal-to, or less-than outcome channel. The image should make the definition feel like a rigorous sorting machine rather than a wall of words. Precise, almost mechanical, with room for later symbolic overlay.

title: Why this is brilliant rather than cumbersome
caption: The genius is that nothing stronger than comparison and repetition is assumed.
role: Reframe the definition as elegant because it uses only what already exists.
prompt_core: Show a complex-looking apparatus built only out of simple pieces already in hand: copied segments, comparison balances, and repeatable stacking. The emotional tone should be one of relief and admiration, as though the reader sees that no illicit multiplication was smuggled in. Think of a disciplined machine assembled from humble components. Serious, clear, quietly beautiful.

title: Easy case and hard case
caption: The hard case is where a deeper definition earns its keep.
role: Contrast commensurable comfort with incommensurable necessity.
prompt_core: Compose a split-panel image. One side shows two segments cleanly measured by a shared smaller unit, everything snapping into tidy integer counts. The other shows a pair like side and diagonal resisting perfect common measurement, yet still being comparable through a higher-order proportion framework. The contrast should feel like mathematics refusing to give up when whole-number comfort ends.

title: The intercept theorem is now earned, not assumed
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: Parallel lines inherit rigor once ratio has been defined properly.
overlay: Exact geometry lines and labels should be overlaid after generation.
role: Show parallel lines transporting ratio after the foundation has been built.
prompt_core: Illustrate two transversals cut by several parallel lines, with corresponding segments on each transversal visibly linked through a calm, earned geometric order. The scene can be staged on parchment or stone with faint triangle areas hinting at the proof underneath. The important feeling is legitimacy: the theorem now rests on prior theory instead of handwaving. Classical elegance, exact linework overlay recommended.

title: Similarity as same shape, different scale
caption: Similarity is shape that survives scale change.
role: Make similarity feel like shape-preserving transformation rather than visual resemblance.
prompt_core: Show one triangle dilating into a larger one through a common origin or through matched angle structure, with their corresponding sides connected by delicate luminous threads. The image should emphasize invariance of shape under change of scale, not merely two triangles that happen to look alike. Crisp, serene, Euclidean, slightly luminous.

title: Historical note: the irrationality shock
caption: The diagonal of the square broke the old comfort of common measure.
role: Capture the conceptual jolt of discovering incommensurable magnitudes.
prompt_core: Create a moody but disciplined scene centered on a square and its diagonal, with measuring cords or rods that keep failing to settle into a common repeat. Let the human figures look absorbed rather than melodramatic. The atmosphere should suggest a crack opening inside an older worldview: arithmetic alone is not enough. Marble, twilight, measured tension.

title: Another lens: ratio as a fingerprint
caption: A ratio can be understood as a structural fingerprint.
role: Provide a modern metaphor for sameness of ratio across different objects.
prompt_core: Show very different objects—a rectangle, a photograph crop, a musical interval waveform, maybe a map enlargement—sharing one hidden ratio pattern rendered as the same luminous fingerprint-like contour or bar-code signature behind them. The metaphor should say: ratio equality is an identity pattern that survives change of medium and size. Modern editorial style, sleek, memorable, not gimmicky.

title: Thought experiments
caption: Keep throwing integer comparisons at the ratios and see whether they ever part company.
role: Encourage the reader to test ratio equality through repeated comparisons.
prompt_core: Create a sparse experimental setup with stacks of copied segments being compared in successive trials, as if a careful thinker is asking over and over whether two candidate ratios ever disagree. The image should feel like a lab bench for pure comparison. Low clutter, repeated forms, meditative rigor.

title: What this section teaches beyond geometry
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
caption: Many deep objects are defined by the full set of tests they survive.
role: Generalize the deeper pattern: define an object by the full set of tests it passes.
prompt_core: Design a conceptual bridge image showing several later ideas—real numbers from nested intervals, derivatives from stabilizing secants, symmetry classes from invariants—all emerging as descendants of one deeper pattern: an object identified by the totality of finite tests it survives. Use repeated visual motifs of filters, narrowing windows, and invariant signatures. This should feel like a revelation about mathematical method itself.

title: Repeated addition is only one shadow of multiplication
caption: Repeated addition is a special case; scaling is the core.
role: Break the school habit of treating multiplication as only repeated addition.
prompt_core: Create a split image. One side shows repeated equal jumps on a line, useful but visibly limited. The other shows a true dilation scene: a shape or segment being resized from an origin, with scale affecting every point at once. The right side should feel deeper and more general, as though the repeated-addition story is only a shadow cast by the richer transformation picture.

title: Dilation: the clean geometric meaning
caption: Scaling means every point keeps its ray and changes its distance by a factor.
role: Make scaling about an origin immediate and bodily intelligible.
prompt_core: Show an origin sending rays outward through points of a shape as that shape enlarges or shrinks along those rays. Every point should remain on its ray, and distances from the origin should visibly scale in proportion. Think of a clean geometric sunburst or optical projector, but elegant and restrained. The image must teach what dilation does in one glance.

title: The similar-triangle multiplication machine
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: Multiplication can be built as a geometric machine for scaling one magnitude by another.
overlay: Hybrid mode essential: use AI base for atmosphere and vector overlay for exact rays, parallels, and labels.
role: Turn the multiplication construction into a memorable device rather than a dry diagram.
prompt_core: Illustrate the similar-triangle multiplication machine on a refined drafting table: perpendicular rays from an origin, the unit point, a point marking a, a point marking b, a slanted connector, and a parallel through a producing the product location. The image should feel like a real mathematical instrument, almost a precision machine for manufacturing products out of geometry. Clean enough for later labels and coordinates to be overlaid.

title: Three meanings that textbooks collapse into one symbol
caption: The same symbol can host different operations; the type signature tells you which world you are in.
role: Visually separate scale-factor times length, length times length, and pure number times pure number.
prompt_core: Design a three-panel image sharing the same multiplication symbol but showing three genuinely different worlds: a dimensionless scale factor stretching a segment, two lengths forming a rectangle of area, and two pure numbers combining abstractly in a clean symbolic register. The panels should rhyme but not blur into each other. This must inoculate the reader against later dimension errors.

title: Why multiplication is commutative
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: The order swap leaves the product invariant because the geometry is symmetric.
overlay: Overlay exact correspondences after generation.
role: Show the symmetry behind ab = ba in the scaling construction.
prompt_core: Create a balanced image where the multiplication machine is mirrored or swapped so that scaling b by a and scaling a by b are visibly the same geometric relation seen from two orientations. The composition should feel like a proof by symmetry rather than a slogan. Use clean intersecting rays and paired triangles, but keep it elegant and spacious.

title: Distributivity becomes geometric
caption: Scaling a whole joined thing equals scaling the pieces and joining them again.
role: Make the distributive law feel like scaling preserving joins and pieces.
prompt_core: Show a single segment made of two joined pieces being dilated, while beside it the two pieces are dilated separately and then rejoined, both yielding the same total result. The composition can echo a folding ruler or extendable instrument. The emotional tone should be inevitability: scaling respects structure and therefore distributes over concatenation.

title: Historical note: geometry and algebra eventually shake hands
caption: Multiplication became richer when geometry and algebra met.
role: Place the geometric and symbolic traditions in dialogue rather than rivalry.
prompt_core: Create a historically layered image in which a classical geometer with compass and straightedge shares a compositional space with a later algebraist working symbolically, their tools and marks converging onto one common construction. Avoid caricature. The image should suggest that geometry and algebra were once different dialects that gradually learned to speak to each other.

title: Thought experiments
caption: Ask what changes under resizing, and what stubbornly survives.
role: Invite the reader to test the resizing viewpoint personally.
prompt_core: Show a quiet set of everyday scale experiences: zooming an image on a screen, enlarging a photocopy, and stretching a simple shape from an origin. Keep the scenes minimal and parallel, so the learner can mentally ask what is preserved and what changes. Sparse, lucid, adult.

title: The single equation behind all division
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
caption: One relation hides under several different division stories.
role: Give one unifying spine for the many faces of division.
prompt_core: Center the image on a single transformative equation rendered visually rather than typographically: an unknown action q acting on b to produce a. From that center, three branches should open into measurement, sharing, and rate. The composition should feel like a master key unlocking three doors. Strong central symmetry, adult editorial style, no classroom vibe.

title: Quotitive division: measurement
caption: Quotitive division asks for a pure count of fit.
role: Show division as asking how many copies of one chunk fit into another.
prompt_core: Illustrate a long segment or rope being tiled exactly by repeated copies of a shorter segment, with the answer appearing as a count of how many chunks fit. Make the result feel dimensionless: the important thing is the count of fit, not a leftover physical piece. Tactile materials, calm lighting, clear repeated chunk structure.

title: Partitive division: fair splitting
caption: Partitive division asks for the size of each equal part.
role: Differentiate sharing division from measurement division.
prompt_core: Show one whole length or loaf-like object being partitioned into equal shares for several recipients, with the visual emphasis on the size of each share rather than the number of shares. The scene should feel fair, precise, and constructive. Use either a rope cut into equal pieces or a straight segment divided with careful craftsmanship; avoid childish pizza clichés.

title: Rate division: the missing third type
caption: When unlike magnitudes are divided, a new kind of quantity is born.
role: Make the unlike-dimensions case feel like the birth of a new kind of quantity.
prompt_core: Create a striking editorial illustration of a journey through time and distance becoming a new object: a slope, a speedometer-like steepness, or a line through the origin in a distance-time plane. The two axes should be visibly different kinds of magnitude, and the result should feel like a new species, not a number sitting on either axis. Sophisticated, concept-first, physically alive.

title: Slope is the geometric picture of rate
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: Rate becomes visible as slope.
overlay: Overlay axes and rise/run brackets after generation.
role: Anchor rate in the visual steepness of a line.
prompt_core: Show a clean coordinate scene where several lines through the origin have different steepness, with one highlighted as the line whose rise over run defines a specific rate. The image should make the slope feel like the embodiment of 'how much per one of the other thing.' Elegant graph geometry, no clutter, optional overlay for axes and rise/run markings.

title: Why the three cases matter
caption: Division is one relation with three very different roles.
role: Prevent the reader from treating all division answers as the same kind of thing.
prompt_core: Design a disciplined triptych where the same outer algebraic shell opens into three outputs of visibly different ontological types: a pure count, a same-kind share, and a new combined-dimension quantity. Use color, material, and spatial logic to make the distinction unforgettable. The image should inoculate against the silent unit mistakes that haunt later math and physics.

title: Historical note: where rate changed the game
caption: Rate division is where mathematics starts speaking physics fluently.
role: Show rate as the door through which physics enters mathematics.
prompt_core: Create a scene that links navigation, falling bodies, and moving planets or projectiles through the shared idea of rate. This could be a collage of a ship log, a clock, a ramp experiment, and a trajectory sketch. The point is that dividing unlike magnitudes let mathematics describe motion and the physical world more honestly. Historical, vivid, not cluttered.

title: Thought experiments
caption: Before computing, ask what kind of answer the division should produce.
role: Encourage the reader to diagnose which world of division a question belongs to.
prompt_core: Show three tiny scenes arranged like cards on a table: measuring how many rods fit into a beam, splitting a rope into equal pieces, and reading speed from a climb or journey. Keep them minimal and parallel so the viewer can mentally sort each one by output type. Clear, sparse, almost like flashcards for deep understanding rather than memorization.

title: The ratio machine
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: A ratio can be constructed geometrically rather than memorized symbolically.
overlay: Exact axes, parallels, and labels should be overlaid after generation.
role: Make the geometric construction of a/b feel like a usable instrument.
prompt_core: Illustrate the ratio machine with two axes, a point representing a, a point representing b, a connector line, and a parallel through the unit point producing the ratio output. Stage it as a beautifully lit instrument on vellum, almost like a scientific device for extracting pure ratios from magnitudes. Clean geometric scaffolding, enough atmosphere to feel memorable, and room for precise overlays.

title: What reciprocal actually means
caption: A reciprocal is the scale that undoes a scale.
role: Replace the ritual word reciprocal with the lived idea of undo-scaling.
prompt_core: Show a segment or image first enlarged by a scale factor and then returned exactly to its original size by the inverse scale, with the second action visually coded as the precise undoing of the first. The scene could use a zoom lens, camera crop, or optical projector returning to baseline. The reader should feel the reciprocal as an inverse transformation, not a numerator-denominator flip chant.

title: Why dividing by a fraction becomes multiplying by its reciprocal
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: The slogan is a consequence of inverse scaling, not the starting point.
overlay: Overlay symbols after generation if desired.
role: Derive the famous rule from inverse actions rather than slogan repetition.
prompt_core: Compose a clear sequential image: a fractional scale factor acts on a magnitude, then an inverse scale restores the unit, revealing why division by that fraction must be multiplication by its undoing factor. The geometry can be hybrid—part ratio machine, part transformation sequence. The important feeling is inevitability: the algorithm falls out of inverse structure. Precise but visually warm.

title: Another lens: zoom factors on a screen
caption: Zoom factors make inverse scaling intuitive.
role: Offer a modern intuitive analogy for ratio and reciprocal.
prompt_core: Show a sophisticated digital zoom interface or camera monitor moving from 100% to 200% and back down to 50%, with the visual emphasis on how one zoom undoes another. The interface should be abstracted and elegant, not brand-specific. The analogy should let an adult learner feel reciprocal scaling in their bones through a familiar modern action.

title: Thought experiments
caption: Think in terms of the operation performed, not only the final quotient.
role: Invite the learner to feel the ratio machine as an action sequence.
prompt_core: Depict a hand moving along a simple ratio instrument, sliding from a known magnitude to the unit point and watching a pure ratio emerge on the other axis. Keep it sparse, like a designer’s sketch of a conceptual tool. The image should invite mental rehearsal of 'what action do I perform, and what kind of thing comes out?'

title: Multiplying by -1 is a geometric action
caption: Multiplying by negative one is the geometry of reversal.
role: Give -1 a concrete transformation meaning: a half-turn or reversal through the origin.
prompt_core: Show a point or arrow on a number line being carried through the origin to the opposite side by a clean 180-degree half-turn arc. The object’s magnitude stays intact while orientation reverses. The image should feel crisp and almost ceremonial, like the simplest nontrivial symmetry in the book. Midnight palette, silver linework, no textual clutter.

title: What (-a)b means
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: A negative factor means scale, then reverse orientation.
overlay: Overlay small arrow annotations if needed.
role: Separate magnitude of scaling from orientation reversal.
prompt_core: Compose an image where a scale factor of magnitude a acts on b, but a second visual layer adds a flip through the origin. Show that the amount of resizing and the orientation reversal are distinct ingredients that can be composed in either order on the line. Clean transformation sequence, strong visual hierarchy, optional overlay for arrows and signs.

title: Squares forget orientation
caption: Squaring erases orientation because two flips cancel.
role: Make the disappearance of sign under squaring feel like double reversal canceling.
prompt_core: Illustrate a left-pointing directed segment undergoing the same flip-and-scale action twice, with the first flip sending it across the origin and the second returning orientation to positive while magnitude is squared. The emotional tone should be clarity: direction information cancels, magnitude survives and compounds. Clean symmetry choreography, almost dance-like.

title: Historical note: the uneasy status of negatives
caption: Negatives felt suspicious until structure gave them a home.
role: Show that negatives were long treated as suspect because their meaning was thin.
prompt_core: Create a restrained historical scene of an early algebraist confronting a negative solution on paper while practical contexts—debts, directions, bookkeeping—hover nearby as the settings that eventually made negatives intelligible. The tone should be thoughtful, not mocking. Candlelight, paper, ink, quiet conceptual tension.

title: Thought experiments
caption: Count the reversals and watch the sign take care of itself.
role: Invite quick mental rehearsal of flip, undo, and double-flip.
prompt_core: Make a sparse sequence with a single arrow on a line, a mirror-like origin, and two successive flips traced in faint arcs. A second tiny panel can show a debt/gain analogy or east/west motion for reinforcement. The point is instant mental replay, not spectacle.

title: Two legitimate meanings of square
caption: Squaring can mean repeated scaling or two-direction area growth.
role: Hold repeated scaling and area scaling side by side without collapsing them.
prompt_core: Create a two-panel image. One panel shows a scale factor acting twice along a one-dimensional line of transformations; the other shows a unit square growing into a larger square whose area changes in two directions at once. The panels should echo each other so the learner feels that both are legitimate meanings of square in different roles. Architectural clarity, tactile surfaces, adult elegance.

title: Why squares appear everywhere
caption: Squares are the signature of two independent directions combining.
role: Connect squares to independent directions and invariants.
prompt_core: Show several contexts where two independent directions matter: a square floor tile, a velocity component diagram, a variance cloud, and a Pythagorean right triangle, all quietly linked by a luminous x-squared pattern of growth in two directions. The image should feel like a conceptual family portrait explaining why squares keep returning throughout mathematics and physics.

title: Square root as an inverse question
caption: A square root asks for the side whose square would produce the given whole.
role: Make root-finding feel like asking for the side that would produce a given square.
prompt_core: Illustrate a known area as a luminous square field or tiled surface, while an unknown side length is being recovered from it like the unlocking dimension of the figure. The scene should feel like de-compression: a two-direction effect being resolved back into one length-like cause. Calm, architectural, with a sense of inverse restoration rather than trick solving.

title: Why negatives vanish under squaring
caption: Opposite directions can generate the same square because orientation is lost.
role: Reinforce that squaring discards directional information.
prompt_core: Show two equal-magnitude directed segments pointing opposite ways feeding into the same resulting square or area block. The transformation should visibly forget the left-right distinction and keep only magnitude. Clean symmetry, muted palette, highly legible composition.

title: Historical note: the diagonal of the square
caption: The diagonal forced mathematics to admit lengths beyond fraction.
role: Historicize the diagonal as the doorway to irrationality and geometry’s authority.
prompt_core: Create a quiet but charged image of a square floor tile or drawn square with its diagonal drawn across it, surrounded by measuring cords that never settle into a common exact repeat. Let the diagonal feel almost like a blade opening a crack in the old arithmetic worldview. Stone, chalk, dusk light, seriousness without melodrama.

title: Thought experiments
caption: Grow the square, then ask what side the area is hiding.
role: Invite felt experiments with area growth and inverse recovery.
prompt_core: Show a small set of tiles growing from a 1-by-1 square to a 2-by-2 and 3-by-3 arrangement, then a separate unlabeled square area asking what side could have made it. Minimal, lucid, almost like a note in the margin of an architect’s notebook. The image should trigger mental calculation by structure, not formula.

title: The tower of operations
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
caption: Exponentiation sits above multiplication the way multiplication sits above addition.
role: Show the ladder from combining moves to scaling to iterated scaling.
prompt_core: Design a visually elegant tower or ladder in three levels: translations/addition at the base, dilation/multiplication in the middle, and repeated dilation/exponentiation above, each level transforming the previous one. The tower should feel like a structural escalation, not a school poster. Use shared motifs so the viewer senses each operation is iteration or inversion of another.

title: Powers are repeated resizings
caption: Powers count repeated resizings.
role: Make exponential growth feel like repeated dilation, not repeated multiplication symbols.
prompt_core: Illustrate a point or object starting at unit size and being repeatedly resized by the same factor, with the spacing between stages visibly exploding for growth larger than one or compressing toward zero for factors between zero and one. The composition should feel like a multiplicative staircase on the number line. Elegant, dynamic, restrained.

title: The exponent laws are structural facts
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: Exponent laws record how repeated scaling compositions behave.
overlay: Overlay small symbolic annotations after generation.
role: Show laws like x^a x^b = x^(a+b) as consequences of composition.
prompt_core: Create a clean transformation diagram where repeated scaling blocks are composed in sequence and then collapsed into one equivalent block with combined count. A second nested diagram can show repeated repeated-scaling leading to multiplication of counts. The image should feel like operational bookkeeping for transformations, not symbolic decree. Crisp, exact, overlay-friendly.

title: Fractional exponents are forced, not invented for mischief
caption: Fractional exponents are required by structural consistency.
role: Make x^(1/n) feel inevitable as the scale that repeated n times gives x.
prompt_core: Show a scale factor machine being asked to run n times and land on a target size x, with the hidden intermediate factor glowing as the only way to make the system consistent. The image should convey inevitability and continuity: the exponent ladder has to extend if its laws are to survive. Sophisticated mechanical-editorial style, no whimsy.

title: Logarithms count multiplicative steps
caption: A logarithm counts multiplicative steps the way division counts additive chunks.
role: Translate logarithms into the question 'how many equal multiplicative jumps?'
prompt_core: Illustrate a multiplicative ladder or path where each step multiplies by the same factor, and the unknown is not the destination but the number of steps needed to reach it. The visual should rhyme with division in the additive world while living entirely in resizing space. Make it feel lucid and unexpectedly human, like counting jumps across scales.

title: The logarithmic line
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: On a log scale, equal spacing means equal ratio, not equal difference.
overlay: Overlay exact tick labels later.
role: Show equal visual spacing standing for equal ratios rather than equal differences.
prompt_core: Create two aligned number lines or distance tracks: a standard one with equal additive spacing and a logarithmic one where 1, 2, 4, 8, 16 occupy equal visual intervals because each step is the same ratio. The key insight should be embodied, not verbal. Crisp analytic elegance, no unnecessary clutter.

title: Historical note: Napier, tables, and the war against drudgery
caption: Logarithms were a computational technology before they were a textbook chapter.
role: Give logarithms their practical historical motive: compressing tedious multiplication work.
prompt_core: Show an early modern scholar or navigator working with astronomical or navigational tables, papers piled high, while logarithmic tables transform a mountain of multiplications into manageable additions. The tone should be energetic but disciplined, like mathematics inventing a power tool against drudgery. Candlelight, instruments, pages, seriousness.

title: Why this chapter matters for calculus
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
caption: Calculus begins when repeated scaling is allowed to become continuous.
role: Bridge growth, exponential structure, and the coming idea of continuous rate.
prompt_core: Design an image where discrete repeated resizings blur into smooth continuous growth, and a curve emerges whose slope seems to echo its own height. The composition should feel like multiplication becoming fluid enough for calculus to grab. Use a subtle transition from stepped structure to continuous curve, intellectually elegant and slightly luminous.

title: Thought experiments
caption: Ask how many equal resizings separate one magnitude from another.
role: Invite the learner to feel multiplicative spacing and counting of resizings.
prompt_core: Show a simple ladder of resizings, a shrinking sequence toward zero, and a side note-like multiplicative jump path on a line. The image should feel like a private scratchpad for testing how repeated factors behave. Minimal, clean, and mentally runnable.

title: Density is not the same as no gaps
caption: Being dense everywhere is not the same as having no gaps.
role: Visually separate infinite crowdedness from completeness.
prompt_core: Create a split image. One side shows a line segment crowded with endlessly many rational markers between any two points, yet with a subtle missing target or hole. The other side shows a complete line where a uniquely determined point is allowed to exist as the limit of refinement. The mood should be conceptual clarity, not mysticism. Deep blue, pale light, disciplined composition.

title: Geometry demands √2
caption: The diagonal exists first; the number system must catch up.
role: Show that the diagonal is a real geometric object before it is a decimal expansion.
prompt_core: Illustrate a simple unit square with its diagonal drawn brightly and undeniably present, while the side sits as the chosen unit. The diagonal should feel physically real and geometrically unavoidable, as though it is demanding a place on the number line whether arithmetic is ready or not. Clean stone or parchment texture, quiet authority.

title: Nested intervals: defining a point by surviving every refinement
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: A real number can be defined by what survives every successful refinement.
overlay: Overlay numeric interval labels after generation.
role: Make the shrinking-interval idea visually unforgettable.
prompt_core: Show a sequence of shrinking intervals on a number line or a corridor of progressively tighter transparent frames all locking onto one single surviving point. The visual should feel like repeated refinement converging without anxiety, each narrower frame increasing certainty. Slightly surreal but precise. Keep room for optional labels like 1.4, 1.41, 1.414 if overlaid later.

title: Connection back to Eudoxus
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
caption: Different chapters are reusing the same deeper method.
role: Reveal the family resemblance between proportion tests and nested-interval completeness.
prompt_core: Design a bridge image linking two seemingly different procedures: on one side, Eudoxus-style comparison tests; on the other, nested intervals squeezing toward a point. In the center, show the shared abstract pattern of an object being identified by all the finite tests it passes. This should feel like a deep structural echo across chapters, almost philosophical but still concrete.

title: Zeno’s shadow and why infinite subdivision does not destroy finiteness
caption: More pieces do not mean more total length.
role: Defuse the intuition that infinitely many pieces must make a length infinite.
prompt_core: Illustrate a segment repeatedly subdivided into halves, quarters, eighths, and so on, with all pieces still visibly reassembling into the same unchanged whole. The image should reconcile the eye: infinitely many finer descriptions do not make the object longer. Calm, exact, almost meditative, avoiding paradox theater.

title: Historical note: rigor took time
caption: The modern real line took centuries to become fully explicit.
role: Historicize the long path from geometric intuition to formal completeness.
prompt_core: Create a visual timeline montage moving from Greek geometric intuition to nineteenth-century rigor, with figures or symbols associated with completeness, limits, and real-number foundations emerging slowly across time. Keep it elegant and spare, not a crowded museum wall. The point is patience: rigor was built, not dropped from the sky.

title: Thought experiments
caption: Let the intervals shrink; watch what remains.
role: Invite the reader to inhabit shrinking intervals and the unchanged whole.
prompt_core: Show a small set of nested boxes, a line segment split finer and finer, and one unwavering point holding still through all the narrowing. The sketch should feel quiet and anti-anxious, as though infinity is being disciplined into something graspable. Minimal, lucid, almost therapeutic for learners who fear the infinite.

title: Why turning deserves its own chapter
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
caption: Moving and resizing were never the whole story; turning completes the trio.
role: Elevate turning to equal dignity with moving and resizing.
prompt_core: Create a compelling bridge image where translation arrows and scaling rays from earlier chapters are joined by a new protagonist: a clean rotating wheel or arm sweeping through angle. The composition should announce that turning is not a side note but another basic mathematical action with its own invariants. Celestial-mechanical mood, serious, elegant, slightly dramatic.

title: Angle is amount of turn, not just amount of corner
caption: An angle measures a turn, not merely a shape at a corner.
role: Break the frozen-school-diagram idea of angle as merely a corner shape.
prompt_core: Show a rigid ray or arm rotating from one direction to another, leaving a luminous swept arc behind it. A corner-like static picture can be faintly present in the background, but the foreground must insist that angle is an amount of turning. Dynamic yet clean, like a precision instrument caught in motion. No text.

title: Radians: the honest unit of angle
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: A radian is built from the circle itself, not imposed from outside.
overlay: Overlay arc and radius marks after generation.
role: Make the arc-length definition of radian intuitive and elegant.
prompt_core: Illustrate a circle where one radius is lifted and then laid along the circumference, showing that the angle subtended by an arc equal in length to the radius is one natural unit of turn. The image should feel honest and inevitable, not arbitrary like degrees. Clean circle geometry, refined composition, optional overlay for arc and radius markings.

title: Sine, cosine, and tangent are shape fingerprints
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: Trig functions are projections and shape relations made visible.
overlay: Exact axes, circle, and projection lines should be overlaid later.
role: Turn trig functions into living geometric relationships instead of SOHCAHTOA chants.
prompt_core: Create a beautiful hybrid image of a point moving around a circle while vertical and horizontal projections drop to form sine and cosine, and a tangent line reveals tangent as a slope-like ratio. The motion around the circle should feel continuous and bodily. Keep the geometry clean enough for later labels, but rich enough to be memorable as art.

title: Another lens: shadows, slopes, and the unit circle
caption: Shadows, slopes, and circles are different faces of one turning structure.
role: Offer multiple entrances into trig for different cognitive styles.
prompt_core: Make a triptych linking a sundial shadow, a hillside or roof slope, and the unit circle projection picture. The same turning quantity should quietly govern all three panels. The goal is cognitive generosity: whichever doorway the learner uses, the underlying structure is the same. Celestial light, clear composition, adult sophistication.

title: Historical note: trigonometry was forced by the sky
caption: Trigonometry was born because the sky and the sea demanded it.
role: Ground trig in astronomy, navigation, and surveying rather than textbook drills.
prompt_core: Create a night-to-dawn observational scene with astronomers, navigators, or surveyors using angular measurements to locate stars, distances, and directions. The circle of the sky and the practical need to project it into numbers should dominate the mood. Historical, luminous, not romanticized into fantasy.

title: Why this interlude pays rent later
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
caption: Turning becomes projection, oscillation, rotation, and eventually waves.
role: Bridge turning to complex numbers, waves, and calculus.
prompt_core: Design a visual bridge where a turning radius on a circle smoothly becomes an oscillating sine wave, a complex-plane rotation, and later a wave or differential-equation motif. This should feel like one idea changing clothes, not several disconnected applications. Elegant continuity, strong visual rhythm, future-facing.

title: Thought experiments
caption: Feel the turn, then watch its projections appear.
role: Invite the learner to physically feel angle, radian, and projection.
prompt_core: Show a person or arm turning through space, a wheel rolling a radius along its own arc, and a shadow length changing as a light rotates. Keep each mini-scene spare and physically believable. The goal is to make trig inhabit the body before it returns to symbols.

title: Why the real line is not the whole story
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
caption: Sometimes the old stage is too small for the action we need.
role: Make the need for the plane feel like necessity, not fancy extension.
prompt_core: Create an image where the number line literally feels too cramped to host certain transformations, while the plane opens beside it as a larger stage. A line-bound world should be shown reaching a conceptual limit, then unfolding into a two-dimensional arena with room for rotation. The emotional tone should be relief and expansion rather than complication.

title: A complex number as magnitude and angle
caption: A complex number can be seen as magnitude plus angle.
role: Introduce complex numbers as vectors with length and direction in the plane.
prompt_core: Show a vector from the origin to a point in the plane, with magnitude rendered as distance and angle rendered as turn. The composition should make polar form feel natural and bodily: how far, and in what direction. Use subtle radial grids or luminous circle hints, but keep the image uncluttered and elegant. Modern, slightly cosmic, deeply readable.

title: Multiplication becomes “multiply magnitudes, add angles”
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: Complex multiplication composes scale with rotation.
overlay: Overlay angle arcs and magnitude marks after generation.
role: Make complex multiplication feel like composition of rotate-and-scale actions.
prompt_core: Illustrate two complex transformations composed in sequence: one rotation-and-scaling followed by another, with the final result shown as a single equivalent rotation-and-scaling. Magnitudes should visibly multiply and angles visibly add. Think of a modernist kinetic diagram or precision watchmaking for transformations. Clean enough for labels later, vivid enough to stick in memory.

title: What i really is
caption: i is the quarter-turn that sends one axis into the other.
role: Give i a geometric body as the quarter-turn.
prompt_core: Show the positive real axis rotating cleanly ninety degrees onto the imaginary axis, with the quarter-turn itself as the central event. The scene should feel like the simplest possible nontrivial complex action: no scaling, only perfect rotation. Minimal, luminous, high-contrast, unforgettable.

title: Square roots of negatives
caption: A square root of negative one is a quarter-turn whose double is a half-turn.
role: Make √-1 feel like the half-turn’s half-turn.
prompt_core: Illustrate a half-turn from the positive real axis to the negative real axis, then visually split that rotation into two equal quarter-turns, the first of which becomes the square root of negative one. The image should make the idea feel elegant and unsurprising once rotation is allowed. Minimal but conceptually sharp.

title: Euler’s formula as compressed geometry
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
caption: Euler’s formula is continuous rotation written in exponential language.
role: Show continuous rotation generating the unit circle and linking exponentials to trig.
prompt_core: Create a stunning but disciplined image where a smooth rotational flow traces the unit circle while simultaneously appearing as exponential accumulation in the complex plane. Cosine and sine should appear as projection shadows or components, not as random floating formulas. The scene should feel like one of mathematics’ great compressions: growth language learning to speak rotation. Modernist, luminous, precise, adult.

title: Historical note: from suspicion to indispensability
caption: Impossible numbers became necessary when their geometry was understood.
role: Humanize the long shift from calling complex numbers impossible to using them everywhere.
prompt_core: Create a sequence moving from skeptical algebra on paper to elegant geometric plane interpretation to modern engineering or physics applications that quietly depend on complex numbers. The tone should be measured and respectful: an idea once mocked becomes indispensable once its geometry is seen. Rich paper textures, modern glow at the end, no triumphal kitsch.

title: Thought experiments
caption: Compose the turns and the scales in your head and watch the law emerge.
role: Invite mental rehearsal of rotate-and-scale composition.
prompt_core: Show a simple arrow in the plane being rotated, then rotated again, while also being stretched. A small unit-circle component shadow can sit nearby. The sketch should feel like a private rehearsal for understanding complex multiplication without formalism. Sparse, exact, mentally runnable.

title: Vectors are directed segments generalized
caption: A vector is the old directed segment grown into richer space.
role: Expand the arrow idea from the line into the plane and space without breaking the original intuition.
prompt_core: Illustrate arrows living in two and three dimensions, all understood as directed segments that can be translated without changing their identity. A few arrows should share magnitude and direction in different locations to emphasize free-vector thinking. Clean spatial engineering aesthetic, subtle depth, no clutter.

title: Magnitude becomes a norm
caption: Norm is the higher-dimensional descendant of length.
role: Show length in higher dimensions as an invariant under rotations.
prompt_core: Create a spatial scene where a vector rotates through different orientations while a glowing sphere or circular shell of constant norm remains fixed. The image should teach that magnitude in higher dimensions is not guesswork but the invariant preserved by rotation. Elegant, high-clarity, restrained palette of steel and light.

title: The dot product measures alignment
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: The dot product measures how much one vector points along another.
overlay: Overlay angle/projection markers later.
role: Make the dot product feel like measured directional agreement, not a mysterious formula.
prompt_core: Show two vectors meeting at an angle, with one casting a projection onto the direction of the other, and a luminous overlap-like quantity indicating alignment. The image should connect angle, projection, and magnitude in one concise composition. Precise linework, calm spatial depth, overlay-friendly for angle marks or projection brackets.

title: Matrices are transformation machines
caption: Matrices are machines that act on entire spaces at once.
role: Embodied matrices as devices that stretch, shear, rotate, and reflect entire space.
prompt_core: Illustrate a simple grid or room being acted on by an invisible machine so that the whole space stretches, shears, or rotates. Include a familiar object like a book or cube so the deformation is felt bodily. The scene should make a matrix feel like an action on space, not a rectangle of numbers. Clean, engineering-grade, concept-first.

title: Historical note: why this language won
caption: This language won because it organizes many worlds at once.
role: Show why vectors and matrices became irresistible across science and engineering.
prompt_core: Create a measured montage linking artillery or navigation vectors, engineering stress diagrams, computer graphics grids, and modern data or physics imagery, all unified by transformation language. The point is not breadth for its own sake but the recurring usefulness of a compact language for direction and change. Crisp, coherent, no clutter.

title: Thought experiments
caption: Move the arrow, rotate the book, and let the structure announce itself.
role: Invite the learner to feel free vectors, alignment, and order-sensitive rotations.
prompt_core: Show a book or block being rotated around one axis and then another in alternate orders, plus two identical arrows moved to different places without changing identity. Add a tiny projection scene for alignment. Sparse and tactile, like a margin note asking the reader to try it with their own hands.

title: Before coordinates, geometry and algebra spoke different dialects
caption: Coordinates built a bridge between shape and symbol.
role: Show the historical gap between shape-thinking and symbolic equation-thinking.
prompt_core: Create a split historical tableau: on one side a geometer working with shapes, compass, and diagram; on the other an algebraist surrounded by symbolic manipulation. In the middle, leave an almost-empty bridge waiting to be built. The image should communicate that analytic geometry was a translation breakthrough between two already powerful but separate languages.

title: A point gets an address
caption: A coordinate is an address relative to chosen axes.
role: Make coordinate assignment feel like choosing an address system, not discovering essence.
prompt_core: Show a point in the plane receiving an address from two perpendicular reference directions, like a city intersection map translated into pure geometry. The point should remain the same object while different frames could, in principle, describe it differently. Clear, map-like, elegant, and adult.

title: Equation as locus, not just a sentence to solve
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: An equation can describe a place, not only a puzzle.
overlay: Overlay specific equations later.
role: Shift equation-thinking from verbal command to geometric set of points.
prompt_core: Illustrate an equation coming alive as a curve or line: countless points satisfying one common constraint and glowing into a visible locus. The image should make 'equation' feel like a habitat of points, not a line of text begging to be manipulated. Clean graph geometry with atmospheric restraint, ready for optional overlays.

title: Solving becomes intersection
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: Solving can mean finding where two worlds agree.
overlay: Overlay exact axes and curve labels later.
role: Show simultaneous solution as where constraints meet.
prompt_core: Compose two curves or a curve and a line in the coordinate plane whose intersection point glows as the place where both constraints are true at once. The rest of each graph should remain visible enough that the meaning of simultaneous truth is felt. Elegant analytic geometry, uncluttered, overlay-friendly for exact graphs if needed.

title: Another lens: machine view and constraint view
caption: The same expression can be read as a machine or as a constraint.
role: Offer two complementary readings of equations and functions.
prompt_core: Design a split image. One side treats a formula as a machine transforming an input into an output. The other treats the same formula as a constraint carving out a shape or locus in space. The two views should visibly share structure rather than compete. Clean modern design, transparent layers, conceptual generosity.

title: Historical note: analytic geometry changed the landscape
caption: Once shape and symbol could translate, mathematics accelerated.
role: Historicize the coordinate bridge as a true reorganization of the subject.
prompt_core: Create a scene inspired by early modern analytic geometry: a room where diagrammatic geometry and symbolic algebra meet on the same surface, with curves gaining equations and equations gaining shape. Avoid portrait cliché; focus on the conceptual merger. Warm paper, precise lines, the feeling of a landscape being redrawn.

title: Why this bridge matters for the rest of the book
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
caption: Coordinates make later function and calculus thinking possible.
role: Make coordinates feel like the hinge from geometry into functions and calculus.
prompt_core: Design a bridge image in which a coordinate grid becomes a curve, the curve becomes a function, the function grows tangents and areas, and the whole analytic world of later chapters unfolds. The point is continuity: coordinates are not a side chapter but the gateway to modern curve-thinking. Spacious, elegant, future-facing.

title: Thought experiments
caption: Ask yourself whether you are reading an address, a machine, or a constraint.
role: Invite the learner to switch between shape and symbolic report.
prompt_core: Show a point being read off a grid, a circle being recognized as the set of points a fixed distance from a center, and two crossing graphs turning into a solved system. Keep it sparse and portable, like a set of private check-your-understanding sketches. Mentally runnable at a glance.

title: The mapping itself is an object
caption: A function is not just what it does; it is also a thing in its own right.
role: Make the shift from action to object explicit and almost physical.
prompt_core: Illustrate a clear but elegant machine that takes in one kind of input and reliably produces one output, while the machine itself is highlighted as the real object of attention. Avoid childish gears; think transparent scientific instrument or sculptural mechanism. The reader should feel that a rule can itself be studied, combined, inverted, and transformed.

title: The graph makes the function visible
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: A graph is the function showing its body.
overlay: Overlay axes and sample points later.
role: Turn mapping into a visible curve in the plane.
prompt_core: Show a set of input-output pairs rising into the plane and cohering into a continuous graph, with the curve felt as the visible body of the function. The image should treat the graph not as homework paper but as the function made visible. Elegant coordinate aesthetics, transparent grid, optional overlay for axes and points.

title: Composition is feeding one machine into another
caption: Composition means do one transformation, then feed its result into the next.
role: Embodied composition as output flowing into the next input.
prompt_core: Create a transparent two-stage machine or pipe system where the output of the first transformation literally feeds the input of the second, producing a final transformed result. The scene should make function composition feel practical and spatial. Clean, glass-like, adult, with a sense of process rather than clutter.

title: Inverse functions and reflection
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: Inverse functions are the same relation viewed with input and output exchanged.
overlay: Overlay diagonal line and axes after generation.
role: Show inversion as input-output swapping and reflection across y=x.
prompt_core: Illustrate a graph and its inverse as reflections across a luminous diagonal mirror line representing y equals x. The swap of input and output should be visible both as mirrored curve geometry and as a conceptual exchange of roles. Elegant and precise, with strong visual symmetry and room for optional annotation overlays.

title: Historical note: Euler and the rise of function-thinking
caption: Functions became powerful when the mapping itself became an object of thought.
role: Place the abstraction of function in its historical emergence.
prompt_core: Create a historical editorial scene where curves, formulas, and mappings begin to be treated as coherent objects in their own right. You can suggest Euler-era notation and paper culture without turning it into a portrait piece. The conceptual birth of function-thinking should dominate, not biographical trivia.

title: Thought experiments
caption: Move between machine view and graph view until they feel like the same thing.
role: Invite learners to think in mappings, graphs, and inverses.
prompt_core: Show a tiny input-output machine, its graph in the plane, and a reflected inverse partner, all in one sparse triad. The point is to let the learner ask: what stays the same when I switch viewpoints? Minimal, clear, encouraging.

title: A polynomial is a constructed transformation
caption: A polynomial is a recipe built from familiar transformations.
role: Make a polynomial feel assembled from shifts, scalings, and powers.
prompt_core: Illustrate a machine assembled from modules labeled only by their visual action: a power-raising chamber, a scaling lever, a shift block, and an addition junction, all combining to generate one smooth output curve. The image should communicate that a polynomial is built from operations we already understand. Editorial-machine style, adult and precise.

title: Roots are where the machine outputs zero
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: A root is where the transformation lands exactly on zero.
overlay: Overlay exact curves later if needed.
role: Turn roots into crossings where the transformation hits the horizontal axis.
prompt_core: Show a polynomial curve crossing the x-axis at several points, with each crossing read as a place where the machine’s output vanishes. The axis should feel like a meaningful threshold, not just graph paper decoration. Elegant analytic illustration, optional overlay for curve specifics and factor hints.

title: The dimension insight: abstract polynomials are usually ratio-machines
caption: Polynomials usually live in the world of pure ratios unless coefficients carry the missing dimensions.
role: Prevent dimensional nonsense by showing why raw lengths cannot usually be plugged into abstract polynomials unchanged.
prompt_core: Create a split image where one side tries to add incompatible geometric species—a length term, an area term, a pure number term—and visibly fails to cohere, while the other side shows a clean dimensionless polynomial machine working on pure ratios. The image should feel like a powerful correction, not scolding. Transparent layers and type discipline are key.

title: Why polynomials matter so much
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
caption: Polynomials matter because they are the first smooth language rich enough to approximate much more.
role: Show polynomials as the simplest smooth curves that approximate richer behavior.
prompt_core: Design a bridge image in which simple polynomial curves act like scaffolding for more complicated smooth behaviors, gradually approximating richer functions. The composition should suggest that polynomials are the training ground and local language of calculus. Clean, elegant, future-leaning, with a sense of increasingly expressive smoothness.

title: Historical note: from Babylon to the Renaissance and beyond
caption: Polynomial thinking has been part of the subject’s backbone for millennia.
role: Give polynomial problem-solving a long civilizational arc.
prompt_core: Create a restrained historical montage from ancient equation-solving on clay or parchment through Renaissance algebraic ambition into modern curve-based understanding. Avoid turning it into a jumble of names; focus on the enduring desire to solve structured symbolic problems and see their geometric consequences. Warm history meeting modern clarity.
