title: Thought experiments
caption: Change the machine slightly and watch where its output hits zero.
role: Invite the learner to imagine turning transformation knobs and watching axis crossings move.
prompt_core: Show a simple curve with a few conceptual knobs for shifting up, stretching, or changing curvature, and watch its zero crossings slide. The sketch should feel like exploratory play for a serious mind, not gamification. Minimal, lucid, graph-like.

title: Calculus did not appear from nowhere
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
caption: Calculus is not a new genre; it is the old scaffold under refinement.
role: Bridge all prior chapters into calculus as a natural culmination.
prompt_core: Create a wide conceptual plate in which earlier motifs—ratio machine, nested intervals, slope as rate, accumulation by pieces, function curves—converge into a single calculus scene. The feeling should be inevitability, not abrupt novelty. This is the moment the reader realizes calculus is the same scaffold under a stronger microscope.

title: Derivative: the slope that survives zooming in
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: The derivative is the slope that remains after enough honest zooming.
overlay: Overlay exact curve, point, and secant/tangent lines after generation.
role: Make the derivative feel like a stable answer emerging from repeated local refinement.
prompt_core: Illustrate a curve under progressive zoom, with secant lines from nearby points settling toward one tangent line that survives the magnification. The image should make the derivative feel discovered through stabilization, not defined by symbolic ritual. Precision optics vibe, elegant analytic geometry, optional overlay for curve and secants.

title: Another lens: local linearity
caption: Local linearity is the visual secret behind derivatives.
role: Offer a gentler intuition: every smooth curve looks straighter up close.
prompt_core: Show a magnifying progression on a smooth curve where each closer view looks more and more like a straight line segment. The image should feel like a microscope discovering hidden linearity inside curvature. Clean, almost scientific-instrument aesthetic, but warm enough to reassure rather than intimidate.

title: Rates and units
caption: A derivative always says 'change in what, per change in what else?'
role: Keep dimensional analysis alive inside derivative thinking.
prompt_core: Design a split or layered image where one derivative is displacement over time, another is area over length, and the output type changes accordingly. The visual emphasis should be that derivatives are rates with units, not abstract slopes floating free of context. Graphs, unit tags, and physical meaning should coexist without clutter.

title: Integral: accumulation under refinement
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: An integral is what survives when accumulated pieces are refined honestly.
overlay: Overlay exact grid/rectangle lines later.
role: Make the integral feel like a stable total emerging from finer and finer pieces.
prompt_core: Illustrate area under a curve being approximated by rectangles that become thinner and more numerous until a smooth accumulated shape is trusted. The image should emphasize accumulation and refinement rather than rote symbols. Precision plus warmth; this is one of the book’s core conceptual images. Optional overlay for axes and partition lines.

title: The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
caption: Accumulating rates and extracting rates are inverse partners.
role: Show derivative and integral as inverse-ish partners in one coherent visual loop.
prompt_core: Create a looped conceptual image in which a curve generates an accumulated-area function, and then the local rate of that accumulated function returns the original curve. Use a thin growing area strip and a tangent-rate motif linked in one elegant cycle. This should feel like one of the grand closure moments of the book—two opposite processes locking together.

title: Taylor series: local information rebuilding the whole
caption: Local information can rebuild astonishingly much of the whole.
role: Show increasingly rich local approximations reconstructing a function.
prompt_core: Illustrate a complicated smooth curve with a sequence of local polynomial approximations: constant, tangent line, parabola, then richer curves hugging the original more closely near a chosen point. The image should feel like zoomed local data being used to rebuild the wider behavior. Elegant layering, clear color hierarchy, no overload.

title: Historical note: Newton, Leibniz, then rigor
caption: Calculus was invented powerfully before it was made fully rigorous.
role: Place calculus inside both invention and later clarification.
prompt_core: Create a disciplined historical montage: geometric tangent and area problems, then symbolic fluency with differentials, then later rigorous epsilon-limit refinement. The image should emphasize continuity more than rivalry. Think intellectual lineage, not duel narrative. Paper, diagrams, motion, measured modernity.

title: Common confusions to avoid
caption: Do not reduce calculus to symbol manipulation divorced from the process it compresses.
role: Kill the usual confusions: derivative is not just 'the formula you get', integral is not just 'area sometimes'.
prompt_core: Design a corrective plate with mistaken textbook shortcuts on one side—derivative as dead algebra, integral as only shaded area—and the fuller structural meanings on the other: stable local rate and accumulated quantity under refinement. The right side should feel like a return of life to the subject. Sharp contrast, sophisticated editorial design.

title: Thought experiments
caption: Zoom in, refine the pieces, and trust the stable thing that remains.
role: Invite the learner to run a small internal movie of zooming and accumulation.
prompt_core: Show a point on a curve under a zoom lens becoming straight, and beside it a strip of rectangles getting finer under the same kind of refining gaze. Sparse, elegant, mentally runnable. The image should feel like a private meditation on what survives refinement.

title: Euclidean geometry already trained us for this
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
caption: Relativity is strange, but it is still geometry organized by invariants.
role: Show special relativity as a change of invariant, not a break from all prior geometry.
prompt_core: Create a bridge image that starts with the familiar Euclidean distance invariant in a spatial grid and then morphs into a spacetime grid where a different quadratic form is preserved. The viewer should feel continuity: this is still geometry, but the preserved quantity has changed. Austere, elegant, deeply structured.

title: Relativity changes the symmetry group
caption: The crucial change is which transformations preserve the world’s law.
role: Make the change from rotations to Lorentz transformations visually graspable.
prompt_core: Illustrate two kinds of preserved structures side by side: Euclidean circles under rotations and hyperbola-like invariant structures under Lorentz-style spacetime transformations. The scene should feel like the rulebook of symmetry has changed while the game of preserving structure remains the same. Minimal, modernist, high clarity.

title: Energy and momentum form a spacetime-like object
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: Energy and momentum belong together as one invariant structure.
overlay: Formula overlay recommended after generation.
role: Make the energy-momentum relation feel like another invariant vector story.
prompt_core: Create a modernist plate where energy and momentum components assemble into a four-dimensional vector-like object, and an invariant relation glows as the preserved 'length' of that object. You need not literalize all four dimensions; instead, use layered projections and a Minkowski-style geometry cue. Precise, restrained, powerful. Optional overlay for formulas later.

title: Why this belongs in the same book
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
caption: The invariant becomes the law, whether in geometry or physics.
role: Show the continuity from earlier invariants to modern physics.
prompt_core: Design a visual family portrait of invariants across the book: translation differences, rotational distances, scaling ratios, complex magnitudes, spacetime intervals, and energy-momentum invariants all arranged as descendants of one shared idea. The image should be explanatory, not merely decorative. The viewer should feel that relativity is a late chapter in the same long story.

title: Historical note: Einstein, Minkowski, and the geometric upgrade
caption: The geometric recasting of physics was an upgrade in language, not mere ornament.
role: Historicize the move from physical principle to geometric language.
prompt_core: Create a historically sensitive editorial scene where messy measurements and physical principles are re-expressed through a cleaner spacetime geometry. The image may hint at Einstein’s physical insight and Minkowski’s geometric recasting without turning into a biographical poster. Modern paper, chalkboards, light cones, disciplined restraint.

title: A brief modern echo: Noether
caption: Symmetry is not decoration; it generates conservation.
role: Tie invariance to conservation law in a single memorable image.
prompt_core: Illustrate a modern, clean conceptual scene in which a symmetry operation leaves an action-like system unchanged and, in a linked chamber, a conserved quantity remains steady. The relation between symmetry and conservation should feel almost magical but structurally crisp. Noether should appear as an intellectual presence, not a portrait requirement. Elegant, compressed, memorable.

title: Thought experiments
caption: Change the frame and watch what changes; then notice what stubbornly does not.
role: Invite intuitive rehearsal of invariant change under changed frames.
prompt_core: Show two observers slicing the same spacetime event pattern differently while one interval remains visually unchanged, alongside a tiny echo of a rotated Euclidean vector whose length stays fixed. Sparse and conceptual, like a notebook experiment comparing two kinds of invariance. Calm, modern, lucid.

title: Build the real numbers rigorously
caption: Rigor is reinforcement, not betrayal.
role: Make the next step feel like reinforcing a bridge pier, not restarting from scratch.
prompt_core: Illustrate the real number line under construction at a deeper level: nested intervals, Dedekind cuts, and Cauchy-like convergence as reinforcing beams under an already-walkable bridge. The image should say that rigor is reinforcement of intuition, not hostility to intuition. Architectural, calm, confidence-building.

title: Re-derive algebraic laws from the geometry
caption: A law becomes trustworthy when you can see what geometry forced it.
role: Frame proof of laws as paying down conceptual debt.
prompt_core: Show familiar algebraic laws etched on translucent sheets hovering above the geometric constructions that generate them—distributivity from scaling joined segments, fraction rules from inverse scaling, sign rules from symmetries. The image should feel like debts being settled and slogans earning their keep. Elegant, scholarly, not overloaded.

title: Go deeper on Euler’s formula and complex exponentials
caption: This is one bridge worth walking farther than once.
role: Present deeper study as thickening one of the most beautiful bridges in the book.
prompt_core: Create a richer version of the Euler bridge where complex exponentials, rotations, waves, and differential equations start to braid together. This should look like an invitation to a deeper chamber, not a rehash. Luminous, modernist, mathematically romantic without becoming cheesy.

title: Differential equations: laws of change become the object
caption: Differential equations ask which curves obey a local law everywhere.
role: Show the next frontier after derivative-as-rate: whole families of curves obeying local laws.
prompt_core: Illustrate a slope field or directional texture in the plane giving rise to many possible solution curves that flow through it. The image should make the subject feel like geometry of laws rather than symbol warfare. Calm, organic, precise, future-facing.

title: Linear algebra as the grown-up form of transformation geometry
caption: Linear algebra is transformation geometry with more serious muscles.
role: Show later study as deepening, not abandoning, the transformation viewpoint.
prompt_core: Create an image where simple arrows and grids from earlier chapters mature into richer linear-algebra structures: eigen-directions, matrix actions, coordinated transformations of space. The tone should be 'same story, larger room.' Clean engineering elegance, strong continuity with section 13.

title: Probability and statistics as structured uncertainty
caption: Probability is not the enemy of structure; it is structure under uncertainty.
role: Open a door from deterministic geometry into distributions, inference, and information.
prompt_core: Show a cloud of data points, a smooth probability curve, and an information-like compression motif sharing one clean space. The image should avoid casino clichés completely. The point is that uncertainty can be structured, quantified, and reasoned about as carefully as geometry. Modern, calm, intellectually inviting.

title: Topology and geometry beyond rigid shape
caption: New mathematics often begins by changing the notion of sameness.
role: Invite curiosity about new sameness relations beyond congruence and similarity.
prompt_core: Illustrate a shape smoothly deforming into another while preserving connectivity, alongside earlier rigid or similarity-preserving scenes fading in the background. The image should make topology feel like a new answer to the old question 'what counts as the same?' Elegant, tangible, not rubber-duck cartoon topology.

title: Abstract algebra: symmetry becomes the object itself
caption: Abstract algebra studies the structure of possible symmetries and compositions.
role: Show symmetry patterns detached from any single geometric carrier.
prompt_core: Create a conceptual scene where concrete symmetries of shapes, equations, and transformations are distilled into a shared abstract pattern—like a crystalline network or operation table without text. The point is that the structure of composition itself has become the thing being studied. Modern, abstract, crisp, not decorative.

title: Topology: sameness under continuous deformation
caption: Topology changes the answer to 'what counts as the same?'
role: Make topological sameness feel like a new, gentler equivalence relation.
prompt_core: Show one shape slowly deforming into another without tearing or gluing, with continuity of the transformation foregrounded. The image should feel serious and elegant rather than novelty-driven. Use tangible surfaces and continuous motion traces so the learner feels what is preserved when rigid shape is no longer sacred.

title: Differential geometry: calculus on curved spaces
caption: Differential geometry asks calculus to live on curved worlds.
role: Show curvature, local flatness, and calculus living together.
prompt_core: Illustrate a smooth curved surface with a tiny tangent plane attached locally, and perhaps a path or field flowing across it. The image should make clear that locally the space can still look linear enough for calculus, even while globally it bends. Clean, luminous, concept-driven, quietly beautiful.

title: Analysis: rigor about limit, approximation, and continuity
caption: Analysis is the culture of taking approximation and limit seriously.
role: Show analysis as the disciplined study of the refinement patterns already present throughout the book.
prompt_core: Create a spare conceptual image composed of nested intervals, convergent sequences, approximation layers, and continuity of curves, all gathered under one austere visual language of precision. The point is not symbol overload but disciplined limit-thinking as a whole domain. Elegant and almost monastic.

title: Probability, information, and computation
caption: Modern mathematics also lives in information, uncertainty, and process.
role: Connect modern mathematical thinking to uncertainty, encoding, and algorithmic process.
prompt_core: Design a contemporary image where probability clouds, encoded bit-like structures, and algorithmic flow diagrams share one coherent space. Avoid tech-bro cliché and glowing nonsense. The visual should suggest that modern mathematics studies pattern, uncertainty, and computation together in deep ways. Crisp, contemporary, intelligent.

title: Why this modern map matters for a recovering learner
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
caption: Advanced mathematics is a landscape you can enter, not a wall built to exclude you.
role: Emotionally reframe advanced math as a navigable atlas, not a wall of alien jargon.
prompt_core: Create a compassionate but serious image of a person or implied viewpoint standing before a richly connected map of mathematical territories—algebra, topology, geometry, analysis, probability—where paths and bridges are visible instead of a forbidding cliff. The tone should be dignifying and invitational, never childish or self-help-ish. This image is about restoring agency.

title: Final thesis
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
caption: Across the whole book, the invariant under the right transformation becomes the real thing to understand.
role: Conclude with the book’s deepest claim: mathematics is organized by objects, transformations, and invariants across every scale.
prompt_core: Create a culminating synthesis image that gathers the book’s recurring motifs—segment, arrow, dilation rays, circle, graph, tangent, complex plane, spacetime grid—into one coherent constellation. The center of the composition should be the idea of invariance under transformation, with every chapter’s object orbiting it. Grand but disciplined, intellectually beautiful, emotionally earned.

title: Cover / title page
caption: From segment to spacetime, one scaffold.
role: Set the tone for the whole book: serious, beautiful, first-principles, transformation-centered, adult, and visually irresistible without becoming sci-fi wallpaper.
prompt_core: Create a cover illustration for an adult mathematics book that begins with a single segment and unfolds through arrow, circle, graph, complex plane, and spacetime grid into a coherent transformation-based cosmos. The composition should feel like mathematics growing from concrete geometry into modern abstraction without ever breaking its spine. Rich but disciplined, elegant typography space at top and center, no embedded text, no classroom cliché, no random equations floating in fog.

title: Front matter opener: school made mathematics feel fake
caption: This book returns mathematics to things you can see and act on.
role: Acknowledge the reader’s experience without pity and signal that this book rebuilds trust.
prompt_core: Show a sterile worksheet-like world of disconnected symbols fading away while, in the foreground, real geometric objects and transformations—sticks, arrows, circles, curves—re-enter as tangible, intelligible things. The mood should be relief and re-entry into meaning, not therapy cliché. Adult, dignified, low-drama, intellectually welcoming.

title: Decoding tool opener: picture, action, relation, compression
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: Every new idea can be decoded four ways.
role: Give the reader the four-lens method in one memorable image.
prompt_core: Design a four-quadrant image around one mathematical idea—such as addition or derivative—shown as picture, action, relation, and compression. Each quadrant should feel different in medium but clearly belong to the same concept: a drawn scene, a transformation process, an invariant relationship, and a symbolic packaging. Elegant, didactic without being textbooky, overlay-friendly for labels later.

title: Permanent scaffold plate
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
caption: Objects, operations, symmetries, invariants: the whole spine at once.
role: Give the whole document a recurring visual anchor that readers can return to.
prompt_core: Create a master visual atlas linking objects, sameness relations, operations, and invariants across the whole book. Segments, directed arrows, triangles, curves, functions, vectors, and spacetime intervals should appear as stations in one transformation-based system. The image should be rich enough to revisit throughout the book but not crowded. Elegant editorial map, adult palette, room for later labels.

title: Roadmap of the journey
layout_label: Bridge plate / wide in-flow conceptual image
caption: This is a journey through one evolving language, not a pile of disconnected units.
role: Make the five-part structure feel like a navigable expedition rather than a syllabus.
prompt_core: Design a five-part journey map that begins with raw magnitudes and ends in modern mathematical landscapes. Each part should have its own terrain character—workshop, instrument room, celestial chamber, analytic city, spacetime atlas—while remaining connected by a single path of transformations and invariants. The tone should be exploratory and rigorous, not fantasy-cartoon.

title: Part I opener
caption: Before symbols, there were magnitudes, comparisons, and choices.
role: Set the tactile, pre-symbolic tone of the first part.
prompt_core: Create a tactile opening image for Part I: wood, rope, brass compass, straight segments, and unlabeled geometric comparisons laid out like the first honest tools of mathematics before symbolism takes over. The mood should be grounded, physical, and quietly profound.

title: Part II opener
caption: Arithmetic becomes transformation.
role: Announce operations as transformations rather than arithmetic recipes.
prompt_core: Create a Part II opener in which arrows, scaling rays, mirrors, and ratio machines appear as a family of operations acting on a common geometric world. The image should feel like entering a room full of instruments that do mathematics by moving, resizing, reversing, and composing. Elegant, kinetic, no clutter.

title: Part III opener
caption: The line widens into circle, plane, and completeness.
role: Signal expansion beyond the rational line into turning, completeness, and the plane.
prompt_core: Create a Part III opener where a number line opens into nested intervals, a turning circle, and finally the complex plane under a night-sky or observatory mood. The feeling should be expansion of stage and depth, not abandonment of prior structure. Luminous, precise, adult.

title: Part IV opener
caption: Curves become objects; change becomes visible.
role: Set the mood for coordinates, functions, and calculus as curve-thinking.
prompt_core: Create a Part IV opener where coordinate grids, curves, machines, tangents, and accumulations flow together like a transparent city of analytic structures. It should feel modern, lucid, and full of motion under refinement. Crisp glassy layers, elegant geometry, no software-dashboard vibe.

title: Part V opener
caption: At the end of the road, the invariant becomes the law.
role: Elevate the final part into an invariants-and-modernity chamber.
prompt_core: Create a Part V opener where Euclidean geometry, complex rotations, and spacetime invariants line up as one family of preserved structures under different symmetry groups. The image should feel austere, modern, and culminating. Black, silver, deep blue, strong structure, no sci-fi noise.

title: Appendix A timeline
layout_label: Hybrid diagram plate; overlay exact geometry/labels later if needed
caption: The subject grew by answering pressure, not by accreting random facts.
role: Give the historical timeline a clean visual spine so it feels like living development rather than trivia.
prompt_core: Design a horizontally flowing timeline mural that moves from ancient geometry and ratio crises through analytic geometry, calculus, complex numbers, linear algebra, relativity, and modern branches. Use a restrained icon system and evolving visual motifs rather than portraits alone. Clean, elegant, highly navigable.

title: Appendix D proof-reading guide
caption: A proof is a path of allowed moves, not a performance of brilliance.
role: Turn proof-reading from dread into a navigable practice.
prompt_core: Create an image of a proof as a path through a structured landscape: assumptions at one end, allowed moves as stepping stones, conclusions at the far side, and common wrong turns faintly visible but not dominant. The image should be calming and procedural without becoming childish. It should tell the reader that a proof is a route to be walked, not a wall to be feared.
